title: Plumbers, Pipefitters, and Steamfitters
description: Plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters assemble, install, alter, and repair pipelines and pipe systems that carry water, steam, air, or other liquids and gases. They work across residential, commercial, and industrial settings, reading blueprints, selecting materials, and ensuring systems meet building codes and safety standards. The occupation requires significant physical dexterity, on-site presence, and applied problem-solving in variable environments.

tasks.name: Install and Assemble Pipe Systems
tasks.description: Cut, thread, bend, and join pipe sections using a variety of tools and joining methods including welding, soldering, and mechanical fittings. Requires precise physical manipulation in confined or elevated spaces.

tasks.name: Review Blueprints and Plan Pipe Layout
tasks.description: Interpret engineering drawings, building codes, and specifications to determine pipe routing, sizing, and installation procedures. Lay out full-scale drawings and plan system configurations before physical work begins.

tasks.name: Diagnose and Repair Pipe System Failures
tasks.description: Identify leaks, blockages, pressure failures, or mechanical faults by inspecting systems, testing with water or air pressure, and using diagnostic judgment. Determine and execute appropriate repair strategies.

tasks.name: Select Materials and Coordinate with Supervisors
tasks.description: Choose appropriate pipe sizes, types, fittings, and supports based on system specifications and site conditions. Communicate progress, issues, and material needs to supervisors, contractors, and team members.

tasks.name: Ensure Safety and Code Compliance
tasks.description: Shut off utilities, use protective equipment, and verify that all installations meet local building codes and safety regulations. Conduct inspections and document completed work for compliance records.

_reasoning.pipe-installation: Highly physical, dexterous, on-site task with no viable remote or digital substitute. physicalBottleneck scored very low (2) reflecting near-total on-site requirement. socialBottleneck high (18) because interaction with clients/supervisors is incidental, not core. E0 tier: AI cannot reduce task time ≥50% due to physical irreducibility.
_reasoning.blueprint-planning: Most cognitively amenable to AI augmentation — involves reading structured documents, applying code rules, and spatial planning. physicalBottleneck scored 18 (partially digital: drawings can be reviewed digitally). E2 because LLM + CAD/BIM tools could significantly accelerate layout planning and code checking.
_reasoning.troubleshooting-repair: Requires on-site inspection and judgment but also benefits from AI-assisted fault-tree analysis and historical repair data lookup. dataIntensity moderate (13) — sensor data and pressure readings provide structured inputs. E1 because LLM alone can assist with diagnosis logic and repair procedure lookup.
_reasoning.material-selection: Mix of data lookup (specs, catalogs) and interpersonal coordination. AI can assist with material selection via structured databases. socialBottleneck low (8) because client/supervisor interaction is meaningful here. E1 for the cognitive/lookup component.
_reasoning.safety-compliance: Routine in structure (checklists, codes) but requires physical verification on-site. AI can assist with code lookup and documentation. routineness scored 14 reflecting procedural nature. E1 for the documentation and code-checking components.
_reasoning.routineTaskIntensity: Calculated as ln(routine_cognitive[~25] + routine_manual[~45] + 0.01) − ln(nonroutine_analytical[~35] + nonroutine_interpersonal[~15] + 0.01) ≈ ln(70.01) − ln(50.01) ≈ 4.248 − 3.912 = −0.41 (slightly routine-leaning but offset by significant nonroutine problem-solving)
_reasoning.complementarityScore: Scored 38/100 — moderate-low. The dominant time share is physical installation (E0, 35%) which AI cannot augment meaningfully. Planning and troubleshooting tasks benefit from AI, but the physical core of the job limits overall complementarity.
_reasoning.timeline: Long-term: physical dexterity requirements, variable job-site conditions, and the need for licensed human accountability mean full automation is distant. AI augmentation of planning/diagnostic tasks will arrive mid-term but won't displace the occupation.
_reasoning.wageEffect: Modest positive (+5%): AI tools will increase productivity in planning and compliance tasks, potentially allowing plumbers to take on more complex or higher-value projects. Physical scarcity of skilled tradespeople also supports wage stability or growth.
